text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** Many {people|人々|/ˈpiːpəl/|Many people live in cities.} have {pets|ペット/動物|/pets/|I have two pets at home.}
**DrJill:** These {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Animals live in different places.} live with people in their homes.
**DrJill:** Pets are part of the {family|家族|/ˈfæmɪli/|My family loves our pets.}
**DrJill:** Some {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Some animals are very large.}, like {goats|ヤギ|/goʊts/|Goats eat grass on farms.} and cows, live on a {farm|農場|/fɑːrm/|We visited a farm last summer.}
**DrJill:** But where do {wild|野生の|/waɪld/|Wild animals live in nature.} animals live?
**DrJill:** Today, Anna helps us answer the question, where do wild animals live?
**Anna:** Hello!
**Anna:** Welcome to my house.
**Anna:** This is a {bat|コウモリ|/bæt/|A bat flies at night.} house!
**Anna:** Last night I saw many {bats|スズキ/コウモリ|/bæs/|Bass swim in rivers.} flying in the night sky.
**Anna:** So I bought this {bat house|コウモリの巣箱|/ˈbæthaʊs/|A bat house protects bats.}
**Anna:** Bats are important {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Animals play important roles.}
**Anna:** You are right, Max.
**Anna:** Bats eat lots of {bugs|虫|/bʌɡz/|Bugs crawl on the ground.}
**Anna:** Many bats live in {caves|洞窟|/keɪvz/|Caves are dark and cool.}, but I do not live near a cave.
**Anna:** So I want to give the bats a safe home.
**Anna:** Bats are {scary|怖い|/ˈskeri/|Scary movies make me jump.}
**Anna:** They are not scary, Daisy.
**Anna:** All animals need food, water, and a safe home.
**Anna:** An animal home is called a {habitat|生息地|/ˈhæbɪtæt/|A habitat is where animals live.}
**Anna:** Say it with me.
**Anna:** Habitat.
**Anna:** Good.
**Anna:** This morning I saw {wasps|スズメバチ|/wɒsps/|Wasps build nests in trees.} in their habitat.
**Anna:** A nest.
**Max:** Be careful, Anna.
**Max:** Wasps can {sing|歌う|/sɪŋ/|Birds can sing beautifully.}.
**Anna:** Do not go near {wasps|スズメバチ/アシナガバチ}.
**Anna:** I am not afraid of {wasps|スズメバチ/アシナガバチ}.
**DrJill:** Anna's {bat house|コウモリ小屋} sounds very cool.
**DrJill:** It is true.
**DrJill:** Wild {animals|野生動物} live in many different places, like {caves|洞窟}.
**DrJill:** They also live in the sea, in {forests|森林}.
**DrJill:** Deserts, and high in the {mountains|山々}.
**DrJill:** Some wild animals live in {zoos|動物園} or nature parks.
**DrJill:** But they are still wild.
**DrJill:** Now let's see some of these places.
**Students:** {Dolphins|イルカ} and {whales|クジラ} live in the sea
**Students:** {Bears|クマ} live in {caves|洞窟}.
**Students:** {Penguins|ペンギン} live on the ice.
**Students:** {Mountain goats|ヤギ} live in the mountains.
**Students:** {Spiders|クモ} live in spider webs, and {honeybees|ミツバチ} live in {beehives|巣箱}.
**Students:** {Birds|鳥} live in {nests|巣} and trees.
**Students:** Some {elephants|ゾウ}, {giraffes|キリン}, and {lions|ライオン} live in nature.
**Students:** Some live in zoos and nature parks.
**DrJill:** Next, we hear from some friends.
**DrJill:** They talk about homes for animals.
**Students:** I like {birds|鳥}.
**Students:** {Birds|鳥} live in {nests|巣} in trees.
**Students:** {Tigers|トラ} live in the {jungle|ジャングル}.
**DrJill:** Thanks friends.
**DrJill:** We just heard about so many different types of animal homes.
**DrJill:** Next, we hear some words that show {location|場所}.
**DrJill:** They help us even more.
**DrJill:** For that we go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** She shows us animals around her home and at the zoo.
**DrJill:** Let's watch!
**Anna:** I am walking through the {zoo|動物園|/zuː/|I love visiting the zoo.}.
**Anna:** I am going to see {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Many animals live in the forest.}.
**Anna:** The {prairie dogs|プレーリードッグ|/ˈprɛri dɔɡz/|Prairie dogs live in underground tunnels.} live under the ground.
**Anna:** {Monkeys|サル|/ˈmʌŋkiz/|Monkeys like to play in trees.} and {orangutans|オランウータン|/ɔːˈræŋəˌtænz/|Orangutans live in rainforests.} live at the zoo.
**Anna:** They walk across a {rope|ロープ|/roʊp/|Children play on a rope swing.}.
**Anna:** {Elephants|ゾウ|/ˈɛləfənts/|Elephants are very large animals.} live at the zoo too.
**Anna:** The zoo has so many {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Animals are fun to watch.}.
**DrJill:** Like us, animals need {places|場所|/ˈpleɪsɪz/|We need safe places to live.} to live.
**DrJill:** Now you can talk about animal {homes|家|/hoʊmz/|Birds make nests as homes.} or {habitats|生息地|/ˈhæbɪtæts/|Forests are habitats for many animals.} in English.
**DrJill:** And join us again next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Anna:** {Animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Animals have different behaviors.} live in many different {places|場所|/ˈpleɪsɪz/|Some places are cold, some are warm.}.
**Anna:** Look, {wasps|スズメバチ|/wɒsps/|Wasps build nests in trees.} live in that {nest|巣|/nɛst/|Birds make nests for their eggs.}.
**Anna:** So come see me again on Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Anna:** Uh
